response.justification: The candidate brings 20+ years of broad software development experience with genuinely strong developer-tooling credentials — test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training, and recent AI-agent tooling — which fits the engineering-productivity focus well. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows an IC-track career with no evidence of formal direct reports, performance management, or organizational change leadership. Worth a conversation only if a management-track transition is acceptable for the role.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and testing-infrastructure experience: authored test runners and assertion libraries, built CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure, created MCP code generators
20+ years of software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, far exceeding the 8-year minimum
Mobile-platform exposure (iOS and Apple TV apps, Swift) plus a track record of teaching and mentoring through workshops and courses
concerns: No formal people-management experience — no direct reports, one-on-ones, or performance management evident, against a 2-year requirement
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or owning a team roadmap at the scope this role demands
Career trajectory is senior/staff IC with no stated interest in or transition toward management

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
